Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Dispersion hardening using amorphous nanoparticles deployed via additive manufacturing

Composition_Info.Note: Nominal composition as 12 vol% B4C (theoretical). Measured nanoparticle composition B3.89C with ~8 at% Cu incorporated (APT); matrix Cu contains ~5 nm diffusion zone.

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized Cu powders (avg ~30 µm) mixed with crystalline B4C microparticles (2-4 µm, 12 vol%) using high-shear dispersion (Nobilta, Hosokawa Micron, 1600 rpm, 20 min, Ar). L-PBF built using SLM machine (BLT S210, single-mode fibre laser 500 W, 1064 nm wavelength, 60 µm spot size) under high-purity Ar (O2 < 50 ppm). Cooling substrate at -20 °C. Continuous scanning with 67° rotation between layers. Volumetric energy density ~180.6 J/mm³ for nanoscale fragmentation.
Process_Text.simplified: L-PBF (BLT S210, 500 W, 1064 nm, spot 60 µm) of Cu+B4C (12 vol%) mixed powder; substrate -20 °C; 67° inter-layer rotation; Ar atmosphere (O2 < 50 ppm); energy density ~180.6 J/mm³.

Microstructure_Text.original: Porosity-free bulk composite (>99.8% density). B4C microparticles melted and transformed into amorphous nanoparticles (~47 nm avg) densely and uniformly dispersed within Cu grains. EBSD shows equiaxed Cu grains, ultrafine 0.39 ± 0.14 µm. HRTEM reveals quasi-amorphous nanoparticles: amorphous matrix with nanocrystalline Cu regions (~2.9 nm). APT shows nanoparticle composition B3.89C with ~8 at% Cu. Limited B/C diffusion into Cu (~5 nm); Cu atoms diffuse into B4C forming nanoscale clusters. NEXAFS B-K edge confirms amorphous structure via broadened π* peak.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Equiaxed FCC Cu grains (0.39 µm) with uniformly dispersed amorphous B4C nanoparticles (~47 nm, quasi-amorphous with Cu nanocrystals ~2.9 nm). >99.8% dense. Strong interface with minimal elemental diffusion.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; strain rate 5×10^-4 s^-1; tensile direction perpendicular to build direction
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Dog-bone, gauge length 10 mm, width 2 mm, thickness ~0.9 mm

Fatigue strength limit >70% of tensile strength at >10^7 cycles; stable cyclic deformation without hysteresis loop degradation

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized Cu powders (avg ~30 µm) mixed with crystalline B4C microparticles (2-4 µm, 8 vol%) using high-shear dispersion (Nobilta, Hosokawa Micron, 1600 rpm, 20 min, Ar). L-PBF built using SLM machine (BLT S210, single-mode fibre laser 500 W, 1064 nm wavelength, 60 µm spot size) under high-purity Ar (O2 < 50 ppm). Cooling substrate at -20 °C. Continuous scanning with 67° rotation between layers.
Process_Text.simplified: L-PBF (BLT S210, 500 W, 1064 nm, spot 60 µm) of Cu+B4C (8 vol%) mixed powder; substrate -20 °C; 67° inter-layer rotation; Ar atmosphere (O2 < 50 ppm).

Microstructure_Text.original: L-PBF Cu-8%-ANP as-built. B4C microparticles melted and fragmented into amorphous nanoparticles (~47 nm avg) uniformly dispersed in Cu matrix. Equiaxed Cu grains with no obvious texture.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Equiaxed FCC Cu matrix with uniformly dispersed amorphous B4C nanoparticles (~47 nm). >99.8% dense.

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.Cu_Yield_Strength_Initial_CPFE: lower than Cu-12%-ANP due to lower initial dislocation density

Properties_Info.Note: Fatigue strength limit >70% of tensile strength at >10^7 cycles

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Multiple stress relaxation tests at engineering strain values of 1%, 2%, 3%, 4%; strain rate 5×10^-4 s^-1; hold time 90 s per relaxation
Properties_Info.Note: Physical activation volume ~20 b^3 for Cu-8%-ANP; comparable to nanograined metals; indicates thermally activated dislocation-ANP interaction with small activation volume

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized Cu powders (avg ~30 µm) mixed with crystalline B4C microparticles (2-4 µm, 4 vol%) using high-shear dispersion (Nobilta, Hosokawa Micron, 1600 rpm, 20 min, Ar). L-PBF built using SLM machine (BLT S210, single-mode fibre laser 500 W, 1064 nm wavelength, 60 µm spot size) under high-purity Ar (O2 < 50 ppm). Cooling substrate at -20 °C. Continuous scanning with 67° rotation between layers.
Process_Text.simplified: L-PBF (BLT S210, 500 W, 1064 nm, spot 60 µm) of Cu+B4C (4 vol%) mixed powder; substrate -20 °C; 67° inter-layer rotation; Ar atmosphere (O2 < 50 ppm).

Microstructure_Text.original: L-PBF Cu-4%-ANP as-built. B4C microparticles melted and fragmented into amorphous nanoparticles (~47 nm avg) uniformly dispersed in Cu matrix. Equiaxed Cu grains with no obvious texture.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Equiaxed FCC Cu matrix with uniformly dispersed amorphous B4C nanoparticles (~47 nm). >99.8% dense.

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.Laser_Absorptivity_pct: >70% (for 4-12 vol% B4C composites at 1064 nm, ~4x higher than pure Cu)

Properties_Info.Note: Fatigue strength limit >70% of tensile strength at >10^7 cycles; stable cyclic deformation demonstrated

Composition_Info.Note: Same starting composition as Cu-12%-ANP but annealed at 1173 K for 6 h to crystallize B4C nanoparticles. Grain size almost unchanged due to nanoparticle grain boundary pinning.

Process_Info.Process_Category: Laser Powder Bed Fusion (LPBF) + Heat Treatment

Process_Text.original: L-PBF Cu-12%-ANP as-built sample annealed at 1173 K for 6 h in glove box (O2 and H2O < 0.5 ppm) to crystallize B4C nanoparticles. Prior L-PBF: Gas-atomized Cu powders (avg ~30 µm) mixed with B4C microparticles (2-4 µm, 12 vol%) via high-shear dispersion (Nobilta, 1600 rpm, 20 min, Ar). L-PBF (BLT S210, 500 W, 1064 nm, 60 µm spot) under Ar (O2 < 50 ppm). Substrate -20 °C; 67° inter-layer rotation.
Process_Text.simplified: L-PBF Cu-12%-ANP (same as Cu-12%-ANP) followed by annealing at 1173 K for 6 h (glove box, O2/H2O < 0.5 ppm) to crystallize B4C nanoparticles.

Process_Info.Equipment: BLT S210 (SLM machine); glove box for annealing

Microstructure_Text.original: After annealing at 1173 K for 6 h, the amorphous B4C nanoparticles became crystallized nanoparticles (CNPs). Grain size remained almost unchanged (~0.39 µm) due to strong pinning by nanoparticles. Higher initial dislocation density compared to Cu-12%-ANP, attributed to thermal misfit upon sample cooling after heat treatment. Dislocation pile-ups observed at Cu-CNP interface upon deformation.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: FCC Cu matrix with crystallized B4C nanoparticles (CNP) after annealing; grain size ~0.39 µm retained; dislocation pile-ups at Cu-CNP interface under deformation.

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.Grain_Size_After_Annealing_um: ~0.39 (nearly unchanged due to grain boundary pinning)
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.Initial_Dislocation_Density_CNP: higher than Cu-12%-ANP due to thermal misfit upon cooling after heat treatment

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; strain rate 5×10^-4 s^-1; tensile direction perpendicular to build direction
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Dog-bone, gauge length 10 mm, width 2 mm, thickness ~0.9 mm
Properties_Info.Note: Cu-12%-CNP as-annealed (1173 K, 6 h); fractures after ~3% elongation followed by sudden failure; necking does not occur before fracture (Considère criterion not satisfied)

Composition_Info.Note: High-purity copper, cold-rolled to 70% thickness reduction, with grain size ~400 nm. Used as comparison reference for Cu-12%-ANP.

Process_Text.original: High-purity copper cold-rolled with 70% thickness reduction, producing grain-refined samples with grain sizes of ~400 nm. This conventional high-strength copper was used for comparison with L-PBF Cu-ANP nanocomposites.
Process_Text.simplified: Cold-rolled high-purity copper (70% thickness reduction) to achieve grain size ~400 nm for mechanical property comparison.

Microstructure_Text.original: Cold-rolled high-purity copper with grain size ~400 nm (similar grain size to Cu-12%-ANP). Used as conventional high-strength copper baseline for tensile comparison.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Equiaxed FCC Cu grains ~400 nm via cold rolling (70% reduction).
